exemplar.title: How does Ottessa Moshfegh use narrative techniques in My Year of Rest and Relaxationto critique conventional perception of mental fulfillment associated with privilege ?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does Ottessa Moshfegh use narrative techniques in My Year of Rest and Relaxationto critique conventional perception of mental fulfillment associated with privilege ?

subject.title: English A Lang & Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Language & Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **the study of language and literature through a wide variety of texts and media from different periods, styles, and cultures**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis of both literary and non-literary texts, examining how language choices create meaning and effect in different contexts**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL) |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL)  |
| **HL Essay** | Written assignment | N/A | 1200-1500 words | 25% (HL only) |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 30% (SL), 20% (HL) |

strengths: The research question is stated clearly and governs every section of the essay with precision.
Topic is communicated accurately and effectively, with coherent sub-headings that mirror the line of inquiry.
Substantive methodological plan is articulated (close reading of narrative voice, temporal manipulation, symbolism) and executed consistently.
weaknesses: Key terms (e.g. âconventional perception of mental fulfilmentâ) remain slightly under-defined at moments, causing minor lapses in exactness.
The methodology discussion is largely implicit and could be more explicit about source selection rationale.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although there are a few lapses in concision and some methodological rationale remains implicit, the focus is sustained with clear purpose, the research question is tightly framed and consistently connected to the analysis, and the methodology is coherent and well executed. These features place the work firmly in the top band (5â6), and the overall clarity and precision justify the full 6 marks.

strengths: Demonstrates strong, coherent knowledge of Moshfeghâs novel and situates quotations within broader debates about privilege and mental health.
Uses subject-specific terminology (e.g. âfirst-person narration,â âtemporal manipulation,â âsymbolismâ) accurately and consistently throughout.
Secondary sources, though modest in number, are applied effectively to support literary arguments.
weaknesses: Engagement with higher-level literary scholarship and theoretical contexts is somewhat limited, relying at times on popular journalism.
Occasional slippage in precision when labeling literary devices (e.g. conflating metaphor and symbolism).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay shows more than âgoodâ knowledge and a consistently accurate use of terminology. While the range of scholarly sources could be broader, the effective integration of primary and secondary materials and the mostly precise deployment of literary concepts merit a high-end score in the 5â6 band, justifying 5 marks.

strengths: Analysis is sustained and well supported by textual evidence, moving through narrative voice, temporal shifts and symbolism in a coherent argumentative progression.
Research is consistently relevant, drawing on a blend of primary quotations and secondary commentary to underpin claims.
The discussion offers a focused evaluation of how privilege is critiqued, leading to a clear, persuasive conclusion.
weaknesses: Alternative readings or counter-arguments are seldom explored, limiting the depth of critical evaluation.
Some theoretical assertions (psychological or social) are accepted with limited interrogation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work operates at the top of the Critical Thinking rubric: research is consistently pertinent, analysis is well supported, and the argument is focused and convincing. Only minor omissions (lack of counter-argument, limited methodological reflection) prevent the absolute maximum, so an 11 out of 12 accurately reflects its near-excellent performance.

strengths: The structure (title page, contents, clear section headings, conclusion, bibliography) follows IB requirements perfectly.
Layout and formatting (consistency of headings, use of italics for titles, in-text citations) are applied correctly and enhance readability.
weaknesses: Citation style shows minor inconsistencies (date formats, missing publication details) that should be standardised.
A formal word count is not explicitly stated on the title page as required by IB conventions.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite a few citation-style inconsistencies and the missing word count, the overall structure and presentation are clearly appropriate, meeting all core IB requirements for layout and navigation. These strengths place the work solidly in the top band (3â4), justifying the full 4 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Strengths**:  The essay identifies primary and secondary sources, outlines an intended analytical approach (close reading of narrative voice, temporal shifts, symbolism, etc.) and provides a bibliography with academic and journalistic materials.  The body sections show some organisation by specific techniques.  

**Weaknesses / limitations**:  The methodology remains largely implicit; there is no explicit rationale for source selection, no discussion of research procedures, and limited critical assessment of secondary materialsâ authority.  The range of scholarly sources is modest and some websites of uncertain academic rigour are included.  

**Judgement**:  These features align with a "mostly complete methodology with generally relevant sources and some evidence of informed selection", matching the 3â4 band.  Given the reasonable though not comprehensive coverage, a score of 4 is awarded.

**Strengths**:  The essay includes a formatted title page, a detailed table of contents, section headings and a bibliography, indicating attention to formal presentation.  Basic academic conventions such as in-text citation and italicisation of the novelâs title are generally observed.  

**Weaknesses / limitations**:  Some IB-specific layout requirements are uncertain or missing in the excerpt: a precise word count is not provided, page numbers appear inconsistently (the table shows numbers but the pages themselves are not visible), and citation style shows irregularities (e.g., mixed date formats, missing publication details).  

**Judgement**:  Because several layout conventions are met but others are incomplete or inaccurately applied, the work falls in the 1â2 band; the stronger end of that band (score 2) is appropriate.

**Strengths**:  The essay draws on a fair spread of supporting materialsâcritical articles, interviews and sociological studiesâto underpin discussion of privilege and mental health.  Most sources relate reasonably well to the research question, and quotations from the primary text are judiciously selected.  

**Weaknesses / limitations**:  The breadth of secondary research is moderate; some sources are of questionable academic standing (e.g., popular websites).  There is limited evidence of deep engagement with established literary scholarship on Moshfegh or narrative technique, so relevance and authority vary.  

**Judgement**:  The overall research base is **adequate to good** and generally pertinent, fitting the 4â6 band; the mixed quality and limited critical depth suggest a mid-band score of 5.

**Strengths**: The essay opens with a clear, engaging title and immediately states a focused research question.  The introduction explains why examining privilege and mental fulfilment in Moshfeghâs novel matters, giving the reader a solid sense of purpose and scope.  The subsequent section-headings mirror the wording of the question, reinforcing the line of inquiry throughout the piece.  

**Weaknesses / limitations**:  Although the overarching purpose is evident, the prose is occasionally verbose and the argumentâs core terms (e.g. âconventional perception of mental fulfilmentâ) are not always defined with precision.  This can blur the exact boundaries of the topic at points in the body chapters.

**Judgement**:  Overall, the topic is communicated accurately and effectively and remains in clear focus for the reader, meeting most descriptors of the 5â6 band, albeit with minor lapses in concision and exactness, so a score of 5 is appropriate.

**Strengths**:  The essay develops a coherent line of argument, moving from societal perceptions to narrative voice, temporal structure and symbolism before reaching a reasoned conclusion.  Evaluation of how privilege fails to guarantee fulfilment is woven through sections and is supported by textual evidence and secondary commentary.  

**Weaknesses / limitations**:  The evaluation remains largely one-sided; it rarely considers limitations of the chosen approach or counter-positions within scholarship.  Some points are asserted rather than rigorously weighed, and the conclusion, while clear, mainly restates findings rather than critically reflecting on implications or methodology.  

**Judgement**:  The work demonstrates a **good level of argument development** but only partial critical evaluation, meeting the top of the 4-6 band.  Hence a score of 6 is awarded.

**Strengths**:  The student offers sustained, text-based commentary on Moshfeghâs techniques, frequently linking quotations to thematic claims about privilege and mental fulfilment.  Paragraphs are generally analytical rather than simply narrative, and there is a discernible argumentative progression from external markers of privilege through narrative voice, temporal structure and symbolism to a concluding synthesis.  Most claims are supported with textual detail.  

**Weaknesses / limitations**:  Analysis tends toward repetition, and some assertions about psychological theory or social context are accepted uncritically.  Counter-arguments or alternative readings are seldom explored, so the depth of critical interrogation is uneven.  

**Judgement**:  These characteristics align with the 7-9 band (âgood analysis with mostly supported conclusionsâ).  Given the solid but not consistently sophisticated critique, a score of 7 is warranted.

**Strengths**:  The essay follows the required structure: title page, contents, introduction, body sections clearly sign-posted with sub-headings, conclusion and bibliography.  Transitions between sections are generally smooth, and internal organisation (e.g., technique-focused subsections) aids reader navigation.  

**Weaknesses / limitations**:  Minor structural issues arise from occasional redundancies and uneven balance between subsections (e.g., lengthy quotation-heavy passages contrasted with brief contextual parts).  Nonetheless, all essential elements are present and logically ordered.  

**Judgement**:  The structure is clearly appropriate and fulfils IB requirements, satisfying the 3â4 band at the upper level; a score of 4 is awarded.

**Strengths**:  The student demonstrates solid familiarity with Moshfeghâs novel, quoting extensively and situating extracts within broader debates about privilege and mental health.  Secondary references, though limited, are generally pertinent and used to frame literary points within social commentary.  

**Weaknesses / limitations**:  Engagement with critical scholarship on Moshfegh or contemporary narrative theory is relatively thin, and some citations (e.g., popular journalism or blogs) lack academic weight.  This constrains the depth and sophistication of the contextual knowledge.  

**Judgement**:  The work shows good but not exemplary knowledge and mostly effective, though occasionally superficial, use of sources, corresponding to the mid-high range (3â4 band).  A score of 4 is therefore appropriate.

**Strengths**:  Literary terms such as âfirst-person narration,â âtemporal manipulation,â âsymbolism,â âimagery,â and âmetaphorâ are applied regularly and usually correctly.  The student is comfortable with critical vocabulary and connects it to textual evidence to advance interpretation.  

**Weaknesses / limitations**:  Precision sometimes slipsâe.g., referring to sleep as both âsymbolâ and âmetaphorâ without clarifying the distinction, or labelling ordinary quotation as âimagery.â  Some analytical claims rely on loosely defined psychological concepts rather than firmly established literary terminology.  

**Judgement**:  The terminology is mostly accurate and appropriate, though not uniformly precise, fulfilling the descriptors of the 3â4 band at its upper end.  A score of 4 is awarded.

**Strengths**:  The research question is explicitly stated on the title page and repeated in the introduction.  It is tightly framed around one primary text (*My Year of Rest and Relaxation*) and a clearly delimited stylistic focus (narrative techniques used to critique perceptions of privilege and fulfilment).  Each body section is mapped directly to aspects of narrative technique, demonstrating deliberate linkage to the question.  

**Weaknesses / limitations**:  At times individual sections drift into thematic commentary (e.g. socio-psychological effects of wealth) without always tying the discussion back to "how" the techniques achieve critique, which lessens absolute precision.  

**Judgement**:  Because the question is clear, focused and generally governs the discussion effectively, the strand merits the upper band, but the intermittent drift justifies a score of 5 rather than 6.

annotations.comment: Weakness: Citation punctuation is inconsistent (e.g., missing commas in parenthetical references). Apply a uniform in-text citation style.
Strength: The analysis of first-person narration is well-grounded in textual evidence, clearly demonstrating how Moshfegh subverts expectations of privileged contentment.
Weakness: Extended paragraphs with multiple embedded quotes can overwhelm the reader. Integrate shorter quotations and focus on precise commentary.
Weakness: Entries lack hanging indents and consistent formatting. Apply a standard bibliography layout for readability.
Weakness: Full URLs are overused. Where possible, cite sources by author, title, journal, and year rather than pasting lengthy web links.
Weakness: The citation â(pmc)â is incomplete and lacks author, date, or title. Provide a full reference to maintain scholarly rigour.
Strength: The title âUnmasking privilegeâ succinctly captures the focus of the investigation, immediately orienting the reader to the specific thematic lens of the essay.
Strength: The table of contents is comprehensive and clearly linked to section headings, facilitating easy navigation of the extended essay.
Weakness: Reliance on a dictionary definition limits scholarly depth. Consider integrating a peer-reviewed source or theoretical framework to define privilege more robustly.

Weakness: The discussion draws heavily on popular websites and journalism rather than academic scholarship. Strengthen the contextual foundation by incorporating more peer-reviewed literary criticism or sociological studies.
Strength: The research question is clearly stated and tightly focused on narrative techniques and critique of privilege, providing a precise foundation for the analysis.
Weakness: The essay lacks a word count at the start, which is a required IB formatting element and helps confirm adherence to the 4,000-word limit.
Weakness: The contextual summary reads as narrative retelling rather than critical framing. Shift towards concise, technique-focused discussion to maintain analytical rigour.
Strength: The context section effectively summarizes the protagonistâs situation, setting a clear stage for the ensuing analysis of narrative techniques.
Strength: The topic sentence clearly links âExternal Markers of Privilegeâ to societal assumptions and aligns directly with the research question.
Weakness: The authorâs name is inconsistently spelled (âOtessa Moshfeghâ vs. âOttessaâ). Verify correct spelling to maintain academic precision and credibility.
Strength: The contextual overview of Moshfeghâs narrative approach demonstrates clear understanding of the primary text and situates it effectively within the essayâs scope.
Strength: The objective paragraph explicitly maps out the methodologies and narrative techniques to be analyzed, providing a coherent research plan.
Weakness: There is a grammatical slip (âThis essay focuses examinesâ). Proofread for clarity by removing the redundant verb.
Weakness: The conclusion restates findings but does not reflect on methodological limitations or suggest areas for further investigation.
Strength: The bibliography is clearly labeled and separates primary from secondary sources, meeting IB structural requirements.
Weakness: Citation styles are inconsistent (mix of DOI, full URLs, missing publication details). Adopt a single recognized style and apply uniformly.
Weakness: The passage is densely packed with long quotations and narrative description. Break quotes into focused examples accompanied by succinct analysis.
Weakness: The reference to âUniversity of Columbiaâ is incorrect; it should be âColumbia University.â Ensure accuracy in institutional names.
Weakness: The argument would benefit from considering alternative readings or counter-examples to deepen critical evaluation of the narratorâs reliability.
Strength: The subheading âTemporal Manipulationâ directly aligns with the research question and signals clear methodological focus.
Strength: The discussion of dark humour and self-destruction adeptly unpacks how satire reinforces the essayâs central argument about privilegeâs emptiness.
Weakness: The passage is heavily descriptive. Increase analytical voice by paraphrasing certain descriptions and focusing on interpretive insights.
Strength: The symbolism of sleep is explored with precise literary terminology and compelling imagery, demonstrating nuanced grasp of metaphorical layers.
Weakness: The discussion of flashbacks would be strengthened by engaging narratology scholarship on temporal structure rather than relying solely on textual description.
Weakness: The use of an <h3> tag for a major technique is inconsistent with earlier <h2> headings. Standardise heading levels for clearer structure.
Weakness: The section heading punctuation (âSymbolism and setting:â) is inconsistent with earlier headings. Remove the colon for uniformity.
Strength: The conclusion synthesises key findings and reiterates the research question effectively, providing closure to the argument.
Weakness: Date formats vary across entries (e.g., 15 May 2021 vs. 18 Dec. 2024). Standardise date presentation to enhance professionalism.
